name: Sierra Gorda Dolmen Circular Route from Valle de
name_original: Dolmen Sierra Gorda — circular desde Valle de la Serena
description: Hike the challenging 13.2-mile Dolmen Sierra Gorda circular route from Valle de la Serena, featuring ancient megaliths and panoramic views.
region_intro: Hiking around Valle De La Serena offers access to a landscape characterized by rolling hills and agricultural plains, with notable archaeological sites. The region features varied terrain, including paths leading to ancient dolmens and historical settlements. Elevation changes are generally moderate, making many routes accessible for different fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Valle De La Serena The most popular hiking route is Dolmen Sierra Gorda, a 2.6 miles (4.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 11 minutes to complete. This easy route leads to an ancient megalithic structure. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cancho Roano Archaeological Trail, a moderate 7.5 miles (12.0 km) path. This trail explores the area around the significant archaeological site of Cancho Roano. Local hikers also love the Sierra Gorda Dolmen Circular Route from Valle de, a 13.2 miles (21.2 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and past historical points, often completed in about 5 hours 55 minutes. Hiking around Valle De La Serena is defined by archaeological sites, rolling hills, and rural paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging, longer routes. The routes in Valle De La Serena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 19 hikers have used komoot to explore Valle De La Serena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Valle De La Serena?
A: Valle De La Serena offers a selection of 12 hiking trails, catering to various preferences. You'll find a good mix of easy, moderate, and some more challenging routes to explore the region's unique landscape.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Valle De La Serena?
A: Yes, Valle De La Serena has 7 easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. A great option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991288849/dolmen-sierra-gorda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen Sierra Gorda&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 2.6-mile (4.2 km) route that leads to an ancient megalithic structure.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Valle De La Serena?
A: The hiking trails in Valle De La Serena are characterized by rolling hills and agricultural plains. You'll encounter varied terrain, including paths leading to ancient dolmens and historical settlements. Elevation changes are generally moderate, making most routes accessible.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Valle De La Serena?
A: Yes, for those who prefer circular walks, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1291223555/sierra-gorda-dolmen-circular-route-from-valle-de" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Gorda Dolmen Circular Route from Valle de&lt;/a&gt; is a notable option. This 13.2-mile (21.2 km) trail takes you through rural landscapes and past historical points, typically completed in about 5 hours 55 minutes.

Q: What historical or archaeological sites can I see while hiking?
A: Valle De La Serena is rich in archaeological sites. You can explore the area around the significant site of Cancho Roano on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293874037/cancho-roano-archaeological-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cancho Roano Archaeological Trail&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991288849/dolmen-sierra-gorda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen Sierra Gorda&lt;/a&gt; route leads directly to an ancient megalithic structure. Other nearby historical sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6088024" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Columns of Zalamea de la Serena&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3162049" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of Sierra Gorda&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or notable landmarks along the trails?
A: Yes, you can find interesting landmarks and viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2458178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Cabra Observation Tower&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views. Other points of interest include the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente la Pared&lt;/a&gt; and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4146077" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de la Pajosa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Valle De La Serena?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Hikers often praise the region for its accessible routes that lead to fascinating archaeological sites and its tranquil rural landscapes.

Q: Are there any moderate trails for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, there are 4 moderate trails for those seeking a bit more challenge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293874037/cancho-roano-archaeological-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cancho Roano Archaeological Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option, covering 7.5 miles (12.0 km) and exploring a significant historical area.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Valle De La Serena?
A: Given the region's climate, spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) are generally the most pleasant times for hiking in Valle De La Serena. Temperatures are milder, and the landscape is often at its most vibrant, making for comfortable exploration of the trails and archaeological sites.

Q: Are there any longer routes for a full day of hiking?
A: For a longer adventure, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1291223555/sierra-gorda-dolmen-circular-route-from-valle-de" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Gorda Dolmen Circular Route from Valle de&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.2 miles (21.2 km) and takes nearly 6 hours to complete. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1294073393/dolmen-sierra-gorda-from-valle-de-la-serena-round-trip" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen Sierra Gorda from Valle de la Serena — round trip&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 8.7-mile (14 km) route.

Q: Are the trails in Valle De La Serena suitable for families?
A: Many of the 7 easy trails in Valle De La Serena are suitable for families, offering gentle terrain and manageable distances. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991288849/dolmen-sierra-gorda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen Sierra Gorda&lt;/a&gt; are good choices for a family outing, combining a pleasant walk with an interesting historical destination.
tips: Population that is close to the Sierras del Arrozao on the one hand and the Río Guadámez on the other. The closest, the Sierra de la Dehesa to the north of the town.

One of the most representative heritage elements of Valle de la Serena is this dolmen, a funerary-type construction of the dolmen culture, in the Eneolithic period. (1,500 BC) It is made with large stones that abound in the place, driven vertically and attached to each other forming a circular mound of about 4 m in diameter which is accessed by a corridor.

faqs.question: What kind of physical fitness is required for the Dolmen Sierra Gorda circular hike?
faqs.answer: This is considered a difficult hike, requiring very good physical fitness. You should be prepared for a trek of over 21 kilometers with significant elevation changes.

faqs.question: Where does the Dolmen Sierra Gorda circular trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting and ending from Valle de la Serena. Parking is generally available within the town, which serves as the primary access point for this hike.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the impressive Dolmen of Sierra Gorda, a significant megalithic monument. From its location, you'll also enjoy expansive panoramic views of the surrounding natural environment.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features mostly accessible paths, but sure-footedness is required. You can expect a mix of surfaces, including sections with abundant large stones and some gravel paths. There are uphill sections, particularly to reach the dolmen, which is situated on a ridge.

faqs.question: Is the Dolmen Sierra Gorda circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact trail are not detailed, generally, dogs are permitted on trails in this region. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially around historical sites and to protect local wildlife. Ensure your dog is fit enough for a challenging, long hike.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Dolmen Sierra Gorda circular route?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit for hiking in Extremadura, Spain, is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these periods, temperatures are milder, making the long and difficult hike more enjoyable than in the heat of summer or the cooler, potentially wetter winter.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee to hike this trail or visit the Dolmen de Sierra Gorda?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Dolmen Sierra Gorda circular trail or to visit the Dolmen of Sierra Gorda itself. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the historical significance of the Dolmen de Sierra Gorda?
faqs.answer: The Dolmen of Sierra Gorda is a representative funerary construction dating back to the Chalcolithic period (around 1500 BC or even between the 5th and 3rd millennium BC). It served as a collective burial site, built from large local stones forming a circular tumulus with a corridor leading to a central chamber.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this challenging hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length (over 21 km), you should pack sturdy hiking shoes, plenty of water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and appropriate clothing layers. A map or GPS device is also recommended, as is a first-aid kit.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail starts and ends in Valle de la Serena, which is a settlement. You can find local amenities, including cafes and accommodation options, within the town before or after your hike.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach Valle de la Serena?
faqs.answer: While specific details on public transport routes are not provided, Valle de la Serena is a town in Extremadura, Spain. It is generally accessible by regional bus services, though schedules might be limited. Checking local transport websites for routes to Valle de la Serena is advisable.
